title: Centos 8 dnf install error
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**

Attempting to install the latest podman on Centos 8, two packages report download errors: catatonit and conmon

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. dnf clean all

2.dnf -y install podman

**Describe the results you received:**
[root@a0ed51e264a8 build]# dnf clean all
57 files removed
[root@a0ed51e264a8 build]# dnf -y install podman
CentOS Linux 8 - AppStream                                                       662 kB/s | 6.2 MB     00:09    
CentOS Linux 8 - BaseOS                                                          1.1 MB/s | 2.3 MB     00:02    
CentOS Linux 8 - Extras                                                          7.4 kB/s | 8.1 kB     00:01    
Copr repo for container-selinux owned by rhcontainerbot                          3.2 kB/s | 1.4 kB     00:00    
Stable Releases of Upstream github.com/containers packages (CentOS_8)             28 kB/s |  69 kB     00:02    
Dependencies resolved.

=================================================================================================================

Installing dependencies:

[root@a0ed51e264a8 build]# dnf clean packages
11 files removed


**Describe the results you expected:**
I did not expect signature or size errors on download

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
<not applicable, since I could not install podman>
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
<not applicable, since I could not install podman>
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
<not applicable, since I could not install podman>
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Centos 8 is in a podman container.

comments.body: @lsm5 PTAL, looks like something about the Cent OBS builds is off?

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@Ds894 @leftl Is this still an issue?  We should have podman-3.0.0-rc1 available I believe.
@jnovy @lsm5 PTAL

@rhatdan still unable to update via kubic repo

> @jnovy @lsm5 PTAL

The stable repo ain't getting any 3.0 until I get green light from matt for v3.0.0

@lsm5 apologies for the late response! Looks like the issue still exists on my VM, even after running `clean all`. I'll try spinning up a fresh install of 8_Stream and give it another go.

I'm updating dependencies on OBS atm. Just sent crun and catatonit updates. I'll check on the rest and try locally as well. Sorry about the lag.

should be fixed with podman 2:3.0.0-3, available on Kubic Stable now: https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/podman

Closing, please reopen if issue persists.

Still seeing this problem tried both centos:8 and  centos:stream8:

```
[MIRROR] containers-common-1-6.el8.noarch.rpm: Downloading successful, but checksum doesn't match. Calculated: 00a3c53eeff9c1144b0b3fa95dd528289cfa3bdb7ba3ff407fdfcf6186afffd4(sha256)  Expected: 00d1ca496f30fca20de3440855a6ffc355b3c61b5c74cd12ef051188792b01eb(sha256)
```

reproducible with this Dockerfile

```
FROM centos:8
#FROM quay.io/centos/centos:stream8
RUN echo "[INFO] Installing Container Tools" \
   && dnf -y module disable container-tools \
   && dnf -y install 'dnf-command(copr)' \
   && dnf -y copr enable rhcontainerbot/container-selinux \
   && curl -sSL -o /etc/yum.repos.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/CentOS_8/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo \
   && dnf clean all \
   && dnf -y install podman skopeo buildah
RUN echo "[INFO] Testing container tools]" \
   && podman --storage-driver=vfs info \
   && echo yes | podman --storage-driver=vfs system reset \
   && podman --storage-driver=vfs --version \
   && buildah --storage-driver=vfs --version \
   && skopeo --version
```
